name: Missing Media Checker
description: Scans your Jellyfin library and compares it against TMDB to identify missing episodes, whole seasons, and movies belonging to collections. Generates a sortable, filterable report accessible from the plugin page.
overview: Finds missing TV episodes and collection movies via TMDB.

versions.changelog:
New Release Calendar tab: upcoming next-episode-to-air dates for every owned series plus future-release movies in box-set collections you already own at least one part of. Month-grouped layout with poster, weekday, TMDB + Jellyfin deep-links. Configurable lookahead (default 90 days) and cache TTL (default 6 h); Refresh button bypasses the cache.

Backend: GET /MissingMedia/Calendar?days=&refresh=, CalendarService (parallel cap 8, cached). TmdbSeriesDetails now exposes next_episode_to_air. SectionContentCache.Invalidate(key) for per-key cache busting.

Drop the DLL and restart Jellyfin.

Removes the script-injection pill that lived in Jellyfin Web's top bar. The pill collided with the File Transformation plugin's gzip-encoded responses (worked around in v2.0.0.1) and added maintenance overhead without much value. Status — last scan time, new missing items — is still on the plugin page. Drop the DLL and restart Jellyfin.

TMDB API key required by every feature. Missing-key state now obvious: orange banner above the input, scan button blocks locally with clear message, scan-failure alert shows real HTTP status + server message instead of '{}'. Drop the DLL and restart Jellyfin.

Tiny diagnostic release. The 'Failed to start scan' alert previously showed an empty {} because jqXHR fields are non-enumerable. Now shows HTTP status code, status text, and response body so the actual error is visible.

## Performance fix

Jellyfin web UI became unresponsive whenever a Missing Media scan was running. Root cause: 16 parallel scan workers each issued their own SQLite query against the same database the UI uses — saturating both SQLite and the .NET threadpool.

### Changes

- **Bulk-load episodes** in one query upfront, then group by SeriesId in memory. Drops *N parallel \`GetItemList(AncestorIds=...)\` queries* down to a single one.
- **Lower concurrency** from 16 → 8 to leave threadpool headroom for UI request handlers.
- **Drop \`Task.Run\` wrappers** around synchronous DB calls.
- **Pre-filter movie scan** so only stand-alone movies (not already mapped via Jellyfin BoxSets) go through the parallel HTTP phase.

### Impact

A scan on a large library used to make the web UI sluggish for the entire duration of the scan. After this change the scan does mostly TMDB HTTP work, which doesn't compete with the UI's DB reads.

### Upgrade

Drop the DLL and restart Jellyfin.

## Changes

- **Removed** "Recently released" and "Recently aired" home-screen sections
- **Renamed** section: "Trending (in library)" → "Trending from your library"

## Also includes (from v2.0.0.1)

- **Fix:** Jellyfin page blank / Safari "cannot decode raw data" when File Transformation plugin is installed — pill middleware now handles gzip/deflate/br compressed responses correctly

## Upgrade

Drop the DLL into your plugin folder and restart Jellyfin.

## Hotfix

**Bug:** Jellyfin web page showed blank / Safari \"cannot decode raw data\" error when the File Transformation plugin is installed alongside this plugin.

**Root cause:** `ScriptInjectionMiddleware` (home-screen pill) was reading FileTransformation's gzip-compressed `index.html` response as raw UTF-8 bytes, producing garbage, then forwarding it with `Content-Encoding: gzip` still set — causing browsers to reject the body.

**Fix:** Middleware now detects `Content-Encoding` (gzip/deflate/br), decompresses before injecting the pill script tag, strips the encoding header, and sends plain UTF-8.

Upgrade: drop `Jellyfin.Plugin.MissingMediaChecker.dll` from the zip into your plugin directory and restart Jellyfin.

## Breaking

Custom Channels are removed. Home-screen rows now register with
[IAmParadox27's Home Screen Sections plugin](https://github.com/IAmParadox27/jellyfin-plugin-home-sections) —
install that first or the section toggles no-op.

## What changed

- Three sections: **Trending (in library)**, **Recently released**, **Recently aired**
- Rows render as card rows identical to *Next Up*, directly on the home page — no more Channels-hub detour
- **Upcoming episodes** section dropped — native Jellyfin TV > Upcoming tab covers it
- Home-screen pill unchanged
- Saved v1.2 channel toggles/names/windows/caps roll forward without migration

## Upgrade

1. Install [Home Screen Sections](https://github.com/IAmParadox27/jellyfin-plugin-home-sections) from its catalog
2. Install this DLL into your plugin directory
3. Restart Jellyfin
4. In each user's home customization, toggle the new sections on

## What's new

### Four optional channels
Surfaced under **Channels** in Jellyfin and addable as home-screen rows via Jellyfin's native home customization.

- **Trending in library** — TMDB weekly trending movies you already own
- **Recently released movies** — library movies released within window (default 30 days)
- **Recently aired episodes** — library episodes aired within window (default 30 days)
- **Upcoming episodes** — non-playable stubs of upcoming eps for series you own (TMDB `next_episode_to_air`, default 60-day horizon)

Per-channel toggles, names, windows, max-item cap and cache TTL configurable in plugin settings.

### Home-screen pill
Small badge in Jellyfin Web's top-right corner showing the count of unacknowledged new missing items. Click → jumps to the report. Injected via `ScriptInjectionMiddleware`; opt-out via the new **Home-screen pill** setting.

### Internals
- `IPluginServiceRegistrator` to wire channels + middleware
- `ChannelContentCache`: TTL cache with thundering-herd guard for TMDB results
- `LibraryBridge`: shared library-query helpers
- `IRequiresMediaInfoCallback` resolves channel items back to real library `MediaSources` for native playback

## What's new

- **Ignore list shows names** — entries now display human-readable titles (e.g. "The Matrix") instead of raw TMDB IDs. Pre-existing entries from v1.1.0.0 fall back to "(TMDB N)" — re-add them once to attach names.
- **Hover-zoom posters** — hovering a poster thumbnail in a group header enlarges it 4× with a soft shadow. Page layout is unaffected while the enlarged image overlays the row.

## What's new

- **New since last scan** — highlights newly missing items since the previous run
- **Season badges** — collapsed series headers show which seasons have missing content (fully missing = red, partial = amber)
- **Poster thumbnails** — TMDB artwork displayed next to each group header
- **Ignore list** — suppress individual episodes, seasons, series, movies, or entire collections; managed from the UI
- **Incremental / smart scan** — skips ended series whose owned-episode count hasn't changed, reducing TMDB API calls dramatically
- **Recently-aired-only mode** — filter results to a configurable day window (e.g. last 30 days)
- **Paginated streaming API** — `/Results/Summary` + `/Results/Groups` endpoints; UI loads pages on demand
- **Plugin notifications** — posts a Jellyfin activity-log entry when new missing media is found

## Requirements

- Jellyfin 10.11 or newer
- TMDB v3 API key
